rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, unambiguous handwriting and no monetary columns on this line.
Clear centered section title under the year, underlined. No amounts on this line.
Description legible. Pounds/shillings/pence columns are well aligned with vertical rules; values 130, 3, 9 are clear and within valid ranges.
Handwriting for description legible. Right-hand numeric trio '3 0 0' clearly aligned in the currency columns.
Horizontal rule above the figure indicates a subtotal/total. Numerals '133 3 9' are clearly written in the currency columns and match the sum of the two entries above.

Description reads as 'Domes payments' (handwritten - the first word could be 'Domes' or 'Donus' but shape strongly matches 'Domes'). Numeric group '1316 . 1 . 3' is reasonably clear and aligns to the pounds/shillings/pence columns; digits are legible.

Description 'Fee fund do' (meaning fee-fund ditto) is legible. Numeric reading '1333 9 9' is plausible but the hundreds-digit formation is slightly stylized (the second '3' cluster has minor ink variation) — chosen reading fits the alignment and is in valid currency ranges.

Description reads 'Deficit Cash Novr' (abbreviated November). Numeric group appears as '1528 . 18 . 9 3/4' — the '3/4' farthing notation is visible after the pence (interpreted as 0.75). Handwriting for fraction is faint/looped, so confidence is moderate.

A right-hand summed figure appears on the same horizontal level as the three previous entries; it reads approximately '2970 3 9 3/4'. The leading and some interior digits are slightly compressed against the vertical ruling and the fraction mark is faint — chosen reading matches visible stroke patterns but has moderate uncertainty.
Description reads 'Domus receipts' (the first word is written similarly to earlier 'Domes/Domus'). Numeric trio '787 5 2' is clearly aligned and legible.
Description 'Fee fund do' repeated; amounts '798 9 6' are clearly written and align with pounds/shillings/pence columns.

There is a subtotal line under the two receipts; the visible digits read '985 14 8' but a faint leading '1' is present above the digit (making '1585 14 8') which also matches the arithmetic sum (787.5.2 + 798.9.6 = 1585.14.8). Leading '1' is faint, so confidence is moderate.

A further aggregated total is written beneath (appears as '1992 10 3/4' or similar). The pence whole digit is faint or partly overwritten, leaving a visible fraction mark interpreted as 3/4 (0.75). The line is quite faint and partially obscured; reading is therefore low confidence.
Date and year written clearly in larger script, centered; no amounts on this line.
Phrase at left preceding signatures, legible though a small ink blot partly covers a letter; clearly a structural/approval annotation not an amount.
Signature line reads 'J. P. Lightfoot' followed by 'Rector'. Handwriting legible; no monetary data.

Second signature line likely 'L. R. Farnell' followed by an abbreviated office (looks like 'Fellow' or 'Fellowr'). The surname's final letters are slightly unclear; identified based on letter shapes but moderate uncertainty.
Third signature reads clearly as 'Charles H. Price' with an office abbreviation similar to 'Fellow.' No amounts present.

A final paragraph of bluish faded handwriting appears at the bottom-left. The text is heavily faded and partially illegible; only the opening words and some letter shapes are discernible. Included as a header/note line (no amounts). Very low confidence in exact transcription.
